query: Boeing 787 737 MAX certificate delegation authority scope conditions FAA approval details 2026

{"query":"Boeing 787 737 MAX certificate delegation authority scope conditions FAA approval details 2026","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1824,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://airmappr.com/news/2026-05/boeing-seven-three-seven-max-seven-ten-faa-certification-on-track","title":"FAA Confirms Boeing 737 MAX 7 & 10 Certification On Track for 2026","content":"In an April 2026 interview, FAA Administrator Bryan Bedford said the agency has not identified any issues that would prevent Boeing from securing certification for the MAX 7 and MAX 10 before the end of 2026.
Boeing confirmed in its Q1 2026 earnings report that the 737 MAX 10 has entered Type Inspection Authorization Phase 2 (TIA2) — the final stage of certification flight testing.
Southwest Airlines, which holds roughly 90% of all MAX 7 orders (289 aircraft), expects FAA approval by August 2026.
Ryanair CEO Michael O'Leary stated in March 2026 that Boeing expects MAX 10 certification in Q3 2026, with first deliveries to Ryanair in spring 2027.
The MAX 10 is particularly relevant for European aviation.
For the full background on the MAX 10 certification process, see our March 2026 coverage.
For pilots preparing for Boeing 737 operator interviews, see our Ryanair Interview Guide and Norwegian Interview Guide."},{"url":"https://skiesmag.com/news/faa-boeing-may-issue-airworthiness-certificates-for-some-737-max-787-dreamliners-under-limited-delegation/","title":"FAA: Boeing may issue airworthiness certificates for some 737 MAX, 787 Dreamliners under ‘limited delegation’ - Skies Mag","content":"Sep 29, 2025 — FAA: Boeing may issue airworthiness certificates for some 737 MAX, 787 Dreamliners under 'limited delegation'. By Skies. September 29, 2025.Read more
Boeing will receive limited delegation from the U.S.
Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to issue airworthiness certificates for some of its 737 MAX and 787 airliners, the agency said.
29, 2025), the FAA announced in a statement on Friday.
In 2022, Boeing lost the right to issue airworthiness certificates for 787 Dreamliners, “because of production quality issues,” the FAA said.
“Inspectors will also observe Boeing’s safety culture, ensuring that Boeing employees can report safety issues without fear of retribution,” the agency said."},{"url":"https://simpleflying.com/how-faa-lets-boeing-sign-off-own-737-max-deliveries/","title":"Here's How The FAA Lets Boeing Sign Off On Its Own 737 MAX Deliveries","content":"Well, since September 2025, the FAA isn't even the entity signing most of those certificates anymore.
The FAA restored limited Organization Designation Authorization (ODA) to Boeing, allowing the manufacturer to self-issue those certificates on alternating weeks while FAA inspectors focus on systemic production-line oversight."},{"url":"https://aviationa2z.com/index.php/2026/07/11/boeing-737-max-7-nears-faa-certification/","title":"Boeing 737 MAX 7 Nears FAA Certification by End of July 2026 - Aviation A2Z","content":"7 days ago — Boeing 737 MAX 7 Nears FAA Certification by End of July 2026. Approval clears finished jets parked at Moses Lake and restores Boeing's authority ...Read more
Approval clears finished jets parked at Moses Lake and restores Boeing's authority to sign off on new 737s.
ARLINGTON— Federal regulators are expected to certify Boeing’s smallest 737 MAX variant before the end of July 2026, closing out one of the longest certification delays in the planemaker’s recent history.
Last year it allowed Boeing to resume issuing some airworthiness certificates for the jets, and the coming decision would extend that trust further.
Boeing has been working to increase output to meet strong demand for new passenger jets.
Chief Executive Kelly Ortberg said earlier this year that certification programs for both remaining MAX models were in their final stages and would finish by the end of 2026.
That timeline collapsed when a 737 MAX 8 crashed in Indonesia in October 2018."},{"url":"https://www.aerotime.aero/articles/32805-boeing-737-max7-max10-certification-deadline-extension","title":"New legislation could extend Boeing MAX 7, MAX 10 ...","content":"Nov 30, 2022 — A United States Senator has drafted new legislation proposing an extension of the certification deadline for the Boeing 737 MAX 7 and the 737 MAX 10.Read more"}]}

The engine of a 737 MAX on the final assembly production line during a media tour of the Boeing factory in Renton, Washington, U.S., April 15, 2026. WASHINGTON, July 17 (Reuters) - The Federal Aviation Administration said on Friday it will allow Boeing (BA.N), opens new tab to issue airworthiness certificates for all 737 MAX and 787 airplanes starting next week, a significant milestone for the U.S. planemaker as it ​ramps up production. The FAA said the \"decision follows months of thorough data and safety review demonstrating consistent production ​quality and reflects the FAA's confidence in Boeing's ability to issue airworthiness certificates ⁠under FAA oversight.\". The FAA revoked Boeing's authority to approve individual MAX ​planes in 2019 after a second fatal MAX crash in Ethiopia, and for Boeing 787 airplanes in ​2022 due to production quality issues. *   About Reuters, opens new tab. *   Media Center, opens new tab. *   Reuters News Agency, opens new tab.","score":0.82186085,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://deeparrival.com/news/faa-boeing-737-max-787-airworthiness-certificates-july-2026","title":"FAA Lets Boeing Resume Issuing 737 MAX and 787 Airworthiness Certificates","content":"# FAA Lets Boeing Resume Issuing 737 MAX and 787 Airworthiness Certificates. Home » News » FAA Lets Boeing Resume Issuing 737 MAX and 787 Airworthiness Certificates. The Federal Aviation Administration will let Boeing resume issuing airworthiness certificates for all newly built 737 MAX and 787 airplanes from July 20, 2026. The decision returns a final production step to Boeing after eight months in which the company and federal inspectors alternated the work and recorded comparable quality findings. This does not mean the FAA is approving the 737 MAX or 787 as aircraft types for the first time. It confirms that a specific aircraft conforms to its approved design and is in a condition for safe operation, and the FAA describes it as the last stage of the production process before delivery. The July 17 decision concerns who may perform and sign the final certificate work on new 737 MAX and 787 aircraft, not whether those existing aircraft families may operate.","score":0.7892503,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.compositesworld.com/news/faa-reinstates-partial-certification-authority-to-boeing-after-safety-review","title":"FAA reinstates partial certification authority to Boeing after ...","content":"SHD Mooresville Facility to Quadruple Cambium U.S. Composites Manufacturing. 29, 2025, the FAA will allow Boeing limited authority to issue airworthiness certificates for some 737 Max and 787 jets following a strict and detailed safety and quality review.
